concepts.title: The upper half-plane model
concepts.blurb: The domain H={x+iy:y>0} is the basic stage for hyperbolic geometry and modular forms.

concepts.title: The hyperbolic metric
concepts.blurb: The metric ds^2=(dx^2+dy^2)/y^2 rescales Euclidean length by height, making the boundary infinitely far away.

concepts.title: Geodesics in H
concepts.blurb: Geodesics are vertical lines and semicircles orthogonal to the real axis; they minimize hyperbolic distance.

concepts.title: SL2(R) action and modular preview
concepts.blurb: Fractional linear maps preserve H and its metric; restricting to SL2(Z) gives the modular tessellation story.

concepts.title: PSL2(R) and the isometry trichotomy
concepts.blurb: PSL2(R) is the full orientation-preserving isometry group of H; its elements split by trace into elliptic, parabolic, and hyperbolic conjugacy classes.
